summary: Defence, IT and pharma leading broad-based momentum rally on Nifty recovery above 23,500.
market_context: Nifty 50 has recovered ~3% in April after the RBI rate cut and easing West Asia tensions. Nifty Midcap 100 is up 7% MTD and outperforming large caps. FII net selling is moderating; domestic-catalyst stocks in defence, IT and pharma are seeing volume-backed breakouts into new technical territory.

stocks.name: MTAR Technologies Ltd
stocks.thesis: Fresh all-time high breakout on a ₹2,395 cr order book and Indian Army's drone roadmap driving defence-sector re-rating.

technical.pattern: All-Time High Breakout (Higher Highs & Higher Lows)
points: Cleared previous 52-week high ₹3,923 by 6.9% — no overhead resistance
Down-sloping trendline breakout confirmed on closing basis with huge volume
MACD, OBV and Bollinger Bands all bullish across all timeframes

fundamental.catalyst: ₹2,395 cr order book; Indian Army released 80-variant drone/UAS roadmap in April 2026
points: Revenue up 136% in 6 months on order inflows from ISRO and defence PSUs
Indigenisation mandate gives long-term revenue visibility
Institutional accumulation; FII + DII combined holding increasing

stocks.risk: Profit-booking at ATH likely; hold only above ₹3,900. Any order deferral is a sentiment risk.

stocks.name: LTIMindtree Ltd
stocks.thesis: Decisive break above multi-month ₹4,380 resistance with rising volumes ahead of Q4 deal-win announcements.

points: Surpassed the ₹4,380 multiple-resistance zone decisively on closing basis
RSI crossed above 50 mark from a trough — momentum inflecting upward
Daily Bollinger Band buy signal triggered; stock above 20-DMA ₹4,245

fundamental.catalyst: Q4 FY26 deal-win pipeline expected strong; USD/INR tailwind with rupee near ₹84
points: Analyst consensus target ₹5,908 — stock trading at steep discount to fair value
IT sector recovery driven by BFSI and manufacturing verticals
Cost optimisation driving margin expansion; expected 150bps QoQ improvement

stocks.risk: Weak Q4 revenue guidance (Apr 28 results) could reverse the move. Key support ₹4,250 must hold.

stocks.name: Natco Pharma Ltd
stocks.thesis: 13-month resistance cleared with 6x volume on first-in-India Semaglutide launch — a strong price-catalyst combination.

technical.trend: Bullish — stock back above all short-term MAs
technical.volume_signal: 6x average on breakout day — extraordinary participation
points: Cleared ₹1,040–₹1,059 resistance band (13 months of consolidation) on 6x volume
RSI at 62 — in breakout territory with room to run before overbought
Nifty Pharma index is YTD flat (-0.1%) vs Nifty -10.8% — sector is a defensive outperformer

fundamental.catalyst: First Indian company to launch Semaglutide injection in multi-dose vials — priced 70% below pen devices and 90% below the originator brand
points: Massive addressable market: diabetes + obesity GLP-1 segment fastest growing in Indian pharma
Business split (demerger) under way — unlocks value for specialty vs generics division
Consistent PAT growth for 6 consecutive quarters; Q4 FY26 results due late April

stocks.risk: Regulatory pricing intervention on Semaglutide possible. Key support ₹1,010 — breach signals failed breakout.
